pls4all/cross-binding benchmark

columns — ▾
density
speedup vs
— rows ⟲ reset
✓ bit-exact (≤ 1e-6) ⚠ drift (≤ 1e-3) ✗ divergent (preprocessing) ⏳ timeout (300s) 42.5 fastest in row (among ✓) click any header to sort · ⇧+click for asc
— —

parity verdict

Each cell shows <median_ms> <icon> vs the reference (pls4all.cpp.ref @ 1 thread).

  • ✓ bit-exact (≤ 1e-6 vs reference)
  • ⚠ close (≤ 1e-3) — algorithmic drift, agrees in practice
  • ✗ divergent (> 1e-3) — different preprocessing default (e.g. tier-2 wrappers use scale=True, externals use scale=False)
  • ⏳ cell timed out (300s wall-clock)
  • — see why a cell is empty

Bold + phosphor highlight = fastest cell in row, only among ✓ cells. ⚠/✗/— never carry the highlight.

why a cell is empty

An empty (—) cell means the backend didn't produce a timing for that (algorithm, size, threads). Four reasons:

  1. External lib doesn't implement the algorithm. sklearn has no CPPLS/sparse-SIMPLS, plsregress is plain-PLS only, ikpls is plain-PLS only, ropls is OPLS only.
  2. pls4all wrapper missing — visible TODO on our side.
  3. Bench script missing dispatch — pure tooling gap.
  4. Run too slow / OOM / crashed — rare; 300s/cell budget.

The CSV's reason column gives the exact code per cell.

host

methodology

  • Reference: cpp.ref @ 1 thread (libp4a via ctypes)
  • Parity tolerance: 1e-6 (per-algo overrides possible)
  • Every backend reads the same orchestrator-generated CSV — bit-identical input bytes
  • 5 runs per cell, first discarded as warmup, median reported
  • Per-cell timeout: 300s
  • Thread control via OMP_NUM_THREADS · OPENBLAS_NUM_THREADS · MKL_NUM_THREADS · BLIS_NUM_THREADS + Context.num_threads (Python) / maxNumCompThreads() (Octave)

See the full methodology page for reference policy and per-algo overrides.

backend definitions

Columns named owner.language[.variant]: pls4all.* are this project's bindings, others are external libraries.

versions
